headline: Minnesota Solar Project Set to Replace Retiring Coal Plant
meta_description: A major solar project in Minnesota will reach 710 MW capacity, replacing a retiring coal plant with clean energy.
summary_short: Minnesota is building a 710 MW solar project designed to replace a retiring coal plant, marking a shift toward renewable energy.
body:
A major solar project in Minnesota has reached 710 megawatts of capacity—enough to replace a retiring coal plant with clean energy generation.

The project represents a concrete shift in how the state powers itself. Rather than rebuilding fossil fuel infrastructure as plants age out, Minnesota is channeling investment into solar capacity, demonstrating that renewable energy can step in at scale to fill the gap left behind.

This kind of direct replacement—solar taking the place of retiring coal—signals a practical path forward for communities historically dependent on coal power. It's not simply about shutting down old plants; it's about ensuring that the same communities have modern, renewable alternatives ready to go.
quick_facts: 710 megawatt solar capacity project in Minnesota
Designed to replace a retiring coal plant
Direct renewable energy transition at scale
